Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a DECLARE model with the following constraints

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The model requires that E must occur after A meaning every trace that has A must eventually have E

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But the noncoexistence rule says that E and C cannot both occur However the intended flow requires both after evaluation E the claim is approved P then notified N and then closed C

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then the model says that E and C cannot coexist so C cannot occur because E has occurred But the existence rule for C requires that C must occur

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Therefore the constraints are mutually exclusive and no trace can satisfy all of them

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The model does not include constraints for activities P and N This is not a contradiction per se but it leaves the process underspecified and may allow deviations from the intended flow

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The precedence rule only requires that C occurs after R However the intended flow requires a sequence R A E P N and then C The model does not enforce the order of the intermediate steps

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Misinterpretation of business requirements The business rule that evaluation E must occur before closure C might have been misinterpreted as a strict exclusion noncoexistence instead of a precedence constraint

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Incremental changes The process might have been changed for example skipping evaluation in some cases but the constraints were not updated consistently

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Technical issues The constraints might have been mined from data that contained errors for example missing events leading to the discovery of a false noncoexistence pattern

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Pressure to close claims quickly The organization might be trying to close claims without proper evaluation leading to a contradictory model

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Claims that have both E and C violating the noncoexistence constraint

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually the model does not have an existence constraint for E it has a responded existence on A But the model does not require that A must occur

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So it is possible to have a trace without A and without E But the intended flow requires A

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Therefore we must also check for claims that do not have A That would be an anomaly because the intended flow requires A

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query for claims that have A but do not have E

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Claims that do not have activity C violating the existence constraint for C

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The intended process assigns an adjuster A and then the adjuster evaluates E So we can check if every E event has a resource adjuster that is in the adjusters table and that the adjuster has the appropriate specialization

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But note the resource field might store the adjuster name or the adjuster id We do not know if the resource in claim events is the name or the adjuster id

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We assume that the resource for E should be either the name or the id cast to string of an adjuster

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This will return E events that do not have a matching adjuster in the adjusters table by either id or name

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The intended flow after A assign the same adjuster should do E evaluate Also the evaluation should occur after the assignment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to find claims where an A event is not followed by an E event by the same adjuster in the same claim

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: The model does not require that the same adjuster does the evaluation But the business logic implies the same adjuster

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Claims that have P but not E This would be a violation of the intended flow

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main anomaly the contradiction makes the model unsatisfiable So we should first check if there are any claims that have both E and C That would violate the noncoexistence constraint Also we should check if there are claims that have A but not E and claims that are missing C

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for claims that have A but not E Check for claims that are missing C Check for claims that have both E and C

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The responded existence constraint for E is triggered by the presence of A So if a claim has A then it must have E Otherwise it is acceptable

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We can also check for claims that have E but not A which would be an anomaly in the intended flow but not in the model

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The init constraint requires that R is the first event meaning that there must be an R and it must be the first

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We can check if the first event per claim is R and also identify claims that have no events
